editableNewDialogue.text: Could you help me tighten up a short script for a virtual meeting? I want it to sound professional but still conversational.
Yes. What’s the goal of the meeting (align expectations, ask for a decision, request support), and how direct do you want to be?
Align expectations and get clarity on next steps. Direct, but not confrontational.

Got it. A simple structure works well on video: (1) quick context, (2) what you’re seeing, (3) impact, (4) what you’re asking for, (5) confirm next step. Want a 60–90 second version or a 3–4 minute version?

Here’s a draft you can read naturally on a call:

“Hi [Name], thanks for making the time. I wanted to quickly align on my current scope and make sure we’re using the support in the way you intended.

From what I’m seeing day to day, most of the workload is centered on administrative HR activities rather than end-to-end recruiting. That’s completely understandable in a busy site environment—but it means there’s limited space for the recruiting support we originally discussed.

The impact is that recruitment activities that would benefit from proactive sourcing and pipeline-building aren’t getting the time they need, while I’m spending most of my capacity on processing and coordination.

What I’d like to agree today is a clear split of responsibilities going forward: which tasks should remain with me, which should sit with the local team, and where you want me to prioritize true TA work. If it helps, I can propose two options: an ‘admin-light’ model where I focus mainly on recruiting delivery, or a ‘mixed’ model with a defined percentage of time capped for admin tasks.

Does that approach work for you—and can we leave this call with a decision on the model and a checkpoint date to review how it’s working?”

If you paste your original text, I can tailor the wording to your exact situation and keep it crisp.
Nice. Can you add one line that makes it clear I’m trying to be solution-oriented, not complaining?

Add this right before your ask:

“I’m raising this because I want to make sure we get the best value from the support—and I’d rather reset the scope early than let things drift.”

[On the Visio call]

You: Iris, thanks for making the time. Just to give you the full picture, right now I’m spending most of my week on contract conversions and follow-ups, and the only requisition I’ve really been able to push forward on the recruiting side is the Exploitant Clean Utilities one.
Yes—and even on that one, I’ve mostly been forwarding profiles from nonstop Consulting because I haven’t been able to carve out real sourcing time myself.

[On Visio]

You: Hi Iris—thanks for making the time.

Iris: Of course. What’s up?

You: I wanted to revisit my scope at Huningue. I came in thinking I’d be driving recruiting support, but most of what I’m doing is HR admin around temp-to-perm conversions.

Iris: I hear you, but I want to correct one point: the assignment to Huningue wasn’t built around giving you “more recruitment challenges.” This site was already in a conversion-heavy phase when we placed you there.

You: So the admin-heavy workload was expected from the start?

Iris: Yes. The priority for Huningue was to stabilize the conversions and keep the process moving. Recruitment is secondary there right now, and that’s why you’re not seeing much external hiring.

You: Okay—that explains the gap. I think I framed it as if the plan was mainly recruiting, when actually the plan was mostly conversions with only limited recruiting when it comes up.

Iris: Exactly.

[On Visio]

You: Hi Iris—thanks for making the time.

Iris: Of course. What’s up?

You: I wanted to revisit my scope at Huningue. I came in thinking I’d be driving recruiting support, but most of what I’m doing is HR admin around temp-to-perm conversions.

Iris: I hear you, but I want to correct one point: the assignment to Huningue wasn’t built around giving you “more recruitment challenges.” This site was already in a conversion-heavy phase when we placed you there.

You: So the admin-heavy workload was expected from the start?

Iris: Yes. The priority for Huningue was to stabilize the conversions and keep the process moving. Recruitment is secondary there right now, and that’s why you’re not seeing much external hiring.

You: Okay—that explains the gap. I think I framed it as if the plan was mainly recruiting, when actually the plan was mostly conversions with only limited recruiting when it comes up.

Iris: Exactly.

editableUpdatedMemories.label: Thoughts/Goals/Long_Term
editableUpdatedMemories.reasoning: The prior memory suggests a long-term goal to expand the role into substantial recruitment beyond admin. In this dialogue, Iris clarifies that the Huningue assignment was expected to be conversion/admin-heavy from the start and that recruitment is secondary, contradicting the premise that the role was intended to focus on recruitment challenges. The updated truth is that the role at Huningue is primarily meant to support conversions, with limited recruitment activity.
editableUpdatedMemories.value: Huningue assignment is primarily conversion/admin support; recruitment is secondary with limited opportunities for substantial recruitment challenges
